title: Pig with a Computer Brain

title: Meitnerium: The Element That Almost Wasn't
story: Zap! In a flash of scientific brilliance, a new element was born. On August 29, 1982, researchers in Darmstadt, Germany, created Meitnerium for the first time. This synthetic element, with atomic number 109, didn't exist in nature. Scientists had to use powerful machines to smash atoms together, forming this ultra-rare substance. Meitnerium only exists for a split second before breaking apart, making it one of the most elusive elements on the periodic table.

title: Krakatoa's Ripples Reach the English Channel
story: Boom! The Krakatoa volcano erupted with such force that its effects were felt worldwide. On August 29, 1883, a surprising discovery was made. Seismic sea waves from the explosion had traveled across oceans, causing the water in the English Channel to rise. Amazingly, this happened 32 hours after the initial blast, showing just how powerful the eruption was.

bonus_fact: Sound waves from Krakatoa's eruption circled the Earth multiple times, detected by barometers worldwide.

Neilson's lightning-fast time of 58.59 seconds secured her place in swimming history.

title: Bizarrap: Argentina's Beat-Making Sensation
